Joinder Of Umpqua Holding Corporation, Dba Umpqua Bank Named As Defendant Doe 1 To The Receiver Kevin Singers Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: JOINDER OF UMPQUA HOLDING CORPORATION, DBA UMPQUA BANK NAMED AS DEFENDANT DOE 1 TO THE RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE

APPEARANCES:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq. Tel:415.563.9300, for plaintiff/opposing party;
Blake Alsbrook, Esq. of ERVIN COHEN & JESSUP LLP, Tel:310.273.6333, for receiver Kevin Singer, who is also present, via ZOOM videoconference;
Robert B. Kaplan, Esq. of JEFFER MANGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP, Tel:415.398.8080, for defendant/moving party UMPQUA BANK, via ZOOM videoconference.

The matter was argued.

At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT UMPQUA HOLDING CORPORATION, DBA UMPQUA BANK'S Joinder to Motion To Strike, as follows:

Defendant UMPQUA HOLDING CORPORATION, DBA UMPQUA BANK's joinder to the motion to strike is granted. The court strikes the second amended complaint without leave to amend based on the reasons articulated in defendant Singer's motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in accordance with the court's tentative rulilng and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

Defendant And Cross Complainant San Francisco Superior Court Receiver Kevin Singers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint And For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar May-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AND CROSS COMPLAINANT SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIROR COURT RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint And For Entry Of Judgment

APPEARANCES:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq. Tel:415.563.9300, for plaintiff/opposing party;
Blake Alsbrook, Esq. of ERVIN COHEN & JESSUP LLP, Tel:310.273.6333, for moving party/receiver Kevin Singer, who is also present, via ZOOM videoconference;
Robert B. Kaplan, Esq. of JEFFER MANGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP, Tel:415.398.8080, for defendant UMPQUA BANK, via ZOOM videoconference.

The matter was argued.

At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendant And Cross Complainant San Francisco Superior Court Receiver Kevin Singer's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint And For Entry Of Judgment, as follows:

Defendant Singer's motion to strike the second amended complaint is granted without leave to amend. The filing is "improper" within the meaning of CCP 436(a).

First, plaintiffs did not comply with this court's order of January 18, 2023, and they missed the filing deadline for the amended pleading by several weeks without sufficient explanation. (CCP 438(h)(4)(A) ["If an amended complaint is filed after the time to file an amended complaint has expired, then the court may strike the complaint pursuant to Section 436 and enter judgment in favor of that defendant against that plaintiff or a plaintiff."]; 438(i)(1)(A) ["If an amended pleading is filed and the moving party contends that pleading is filed after the time to file an amended pleading has expired or that the pleading is in violation of the court's prior ruling on the motion, then that party shall move to strike the pleading and enter judgment in its favor."].)

Second, the doctrines of claim and issue preclusion bar this action. (DKN Holdings LLC v. Faerber (2015) 61 Cal.4th 813, 824 [noting preference for the terms "claim preclusion" and "issue preclusion" instead of "res judicata" and "collateral estoppel"].) This action attempts to re-litigate issues that were finally and adversely determined against plaintiffs in Umpqua Bank v. Xelan Prop 1, LLC (CGC-18-564941). Plaintiff's remedy was to pursue their appeal of the August 3, 2021, judgment in that action rather than collaterally attacking it in this action.

Counsel for the prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in accordance with the court's tentative rulilng and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
